briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware company Synapse, highlighting systemic risks in the BaaS model.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's deepening interest in financial data and credit services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping payments competition and what it means for consumers and incumbents alike.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is pushing credit cards into new territory with unusual reward structures, embedded features, and niche targeting that challenge traditional card models.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Global payroll platform Borderless AI now lets employees get paid in crypto](https://betakit.com/global-payroll-platform-borderless-ai-now-lets-employees-get-paid-in-crypto/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian global payroll platform Borderless AI has added cryptocurrency payment options, allowing employees worldwide to receive their wages in digital assets.

- **[BDC's new $500-million loan program will help smaller businesses adopt AI](https://betakit.com/bdcs-new-500-million-loan-program-will-help-smaller-businesses-adopt-ai/)** — *BetaKit* — The Business Development Bank of Canada is launching a $500-million loan program to help smaller businesses integrate artificial intelligence into their operations.

- **[We ran the numbers on Canada's AI consultation. The results show a divided public](https://betakit.com/we-ran-the-numbers-on-canadas-ai-consultation-the-results-show-a-divided-public/)** — *BetaKit* — An analysis of Canada's public AI consultation reveals deep divisions among Canadians on how AI should be regulated and governed.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's open banking advisory committee declared screen scraping insecure and set a target for implementing a formal open banking framework, a foundational step still reverberating in today's policy conversations.

- **[Embargoed Technical Briefing: Canada's Top Moonshot Ventures™ of 2026 Announced at NACO Summit](https://financialpost.com/globe-newswire/embargoed-technical-briefing-canadas-top-moonshot-ventures-of-2026-announced-at-naco-summit)** — *Financial Post* — The National Angel Capital Organization will host a briefing on May 1 ahead of publicly announcing Canada's top high-growth ventures at the NACO Summit in Ottawa, spotlighting the country's most ambitious startups.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Cohere to acquire Germany's Aleph Alpha in sovereign AI play](https://betakit.com/cohere-to-acquire-germanys-aleph-alpha-in-sovereign-ai-play/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian AI company Cohere's acquisition of Germany's Aleph Alpha underscores the growing importance of sovereign AI infrastructure, a trend with direct implications for how Atlantic Canadian fintechs and financial institutions approach AI adoption and data sovereignty.

- **[Posthaste: Deficits are climbing across Canada, but this province is awash in red ink](https://financialpost.com/news/posthaste-deficits-are-climbing-across-canada-but-this-province-is-awash-in-red-ink)** — *Financial Post* — With provincial deficits rising across Canada — including in Atlantic provinces — the fiscal pressures on regional governments could shape funding priorities for local tech and fintech ecosystem development.
